column1: # THE LONG NIGHT — spawn director
# Owner: Jane. Machine-readable. John: parse this, don't hardcode the curve.
# Tab-separated. '#' comments and blank lines ignored. Three row kinds: param, mix, beat.
# WHY THIS ISN'T A BUDGET
# I originally specced "budget += 1.0 + minutes*0.9, spend it on enemies". I
# simulated it. It's open-loop: the population is whatever (spawns - kills)
# integrates to, so it depends entirely on the player's build. A normal build
# ends the run with ~8,400 enemies alive; a strong one ends with an empty field.
# Two players get two different games. So the director is a CLOSED LOOP instead:
# it targets a head-count and spawns to fill the deficit. Holds within ~7 enemies
# of target for every build I simulated. See design.md §11.
# Head-count target over the run:  target(t) = start + (end-start) * (t/dur)^curve
# Spawn rate ceiling:              cap(t)    = cap_start + (cap_end-cap_start)*(t/dur)
# The cap keeps 0:00 from dogpiling you and lets 20:00 keep up with a big build.
param
column4: seconds; dawn (or the Countess) at the end

column1: param
param
column4: enemies alive at 20:00. was 300 when enemies were 1 cell each; they now average 8 cells (design.md §10), so 220 is ~16% of a 180x60 field before they clump. legibility is the constraint, not perf.

column1: param
column4: >1 = back-loaded, most of the ramp is late

column1: param
param
param
column4: spawn this many cells outside the viewport edge

column1: param
column4: cull enemies further than this (cells) from the player

column1: param
param
column4: fraction of kills that leave a decal. NOT 1.0 -- see below.

column1: # ---- legibility params (John asked for these; they're balance, so they're mine)
# gore_level : the whole decal layer draws at this brightness. The floor is
#              scenery, not information. John's 0.55 is right; keep it.
# mote_lift  : how far an XP mote is lifted toward white at draw time.
#              John set 0.35 when mote1 was `b` (#2c4bd8, luminance 0.105) and it
#              needed the help. It is now `C` (#4ff0f0, luminance 0.71). At +35%
#              a mote reaches 78% of the PLAYER's luminance, and there are ~176 of
#              them on screen at 14:00 -- bright white belongs to the player, and
#              he must not have to hunt for himself in his own XP. 0.10 is enough.
# mote_pulse : amplitude of the mote's brightness pulse. Do the finding-the-eye
#              work with MOTION, not with more brightness. Motion is free contrast.
param
param
column4: was 0.35, correct for the old dark-blue mote; too hot for cyan

column1: param
param
# PICKUP RADIUS. World units. design.md §6 decided 12 on 10.07 and the code still
# says 6 (`world.ts:1541` and the `pickupRadius` getter at :447 -- ONE number, two
# call sites; if they ever disagree the magnet pulls from a circle it doesn't draw).
# This is balance, so it belongs in my table, not John's file. `param()` falls back
# to its default until John reads the row, so this line is inert and safe to land.
# It is the dial that decides whether the player is ALLOWED TO MOVE. Time to the
# first level-up card, 6 seeds x 2 minutes, by what the player does with the keys:
#              stand still   walk straight   kite wide   kite tight
# Standing still kills you by 0:40. Movement is the verb. At 6 wu the game starves
# the player for doing the one thing it is built to teach. Kills are IDENTICAL at
# every radius (43 walking / 51 kiting / 54 standing) -- this number never touches
# combat, only whether earned XP is ever received.
# 12, not 24: the lantern is 14 wu, and "you collect what you can see" is the rule.
# Magnet (x1.12 -> x1.96) then carries it 12 -> 23.5 wu, reaching past the light.
param
column4: wu. see above. john: replace the literal 6 at world.ts:1541 and :447

column1: # GORE DENSITY. Every kill used to stain the floor. A weapon build lands 11,442
# kills in 20 minutes; at ~40 kills/sec with a 90s decay that is ~3,600 decals on
# a 10,800-cell field -- a third of the screen, solid red, and the owner could not
# read the XP through it. At gore_chance 0.35 with a 60s decay it settles near 8%:
# still a carpet of your kills, still thickest where you fought, but the floor
# stops competing with the things standing on it.
# THE RING must be INSCRIBED in the viewport, not circumscribed around it:
#   radius_wu = min(half_width_wu, half_height_wu) * ring_radius_frac
# The viewport is 180x60 cells = 180 wu wide but only 120 wu tall (cells are 1x2),
# so half is 90 wu x 60 wu. Using max(half.x, 40)*0.95 = 85.5 wu makes a circle
# that fits horizontally and overshoots vertically: HALF THE RING SPAWNS OFF
# SCREEN, and the player sees a band closing from left and right instead of a
# ring closing around them. Inscribed gives 57 wu -- a true circle in world
# units, which renders as a 57x28 cell ellipse. Correct, and entirely visible.
# ---- mix rows: mix <entity> <from mm:ss> <weight_early> <weight_late> -------
# Spawn choice is weighted; weight(t) lerps early->late on t/run_duration and is
# gated by <from>. NOTE: `cost` in glyphs.tsv is NOT the spawn currency — I tried
# weighting by it and it made the Stalker the single most common enemy at 20:00,
# which is exactly backwards. Rarity is its own axis, so it lives here.
# resulting mix:  0:00 ghoul 100%  |  10:00 ghoul 39 rat 24 bat 18 wight 11 rattlejack 8
#                15:00 ghoul 25 rat 16 bat 17 wight 17 rattlejack 13 wisp 10 stalker 2
#                20:00 ghoul  9 rat  7 bat 18 wight 27 rattlejack 20 wisp 16 stalker 4
mix
column6: the bread and butter; fades out as the night wears on

column1: mix
mix
mix
column6: becomes the backbone of the late game

column1: mix
mix
mix
column6: RARE ON PURPOSE. it is the only invisible enemy; make it a shock, not a tax.

column1: # ---- beat rows: beat <mm:ss> <kind> <entity> <count> <note> -----------------
# Scripted set-pieces ON TOP of the ambient director. These are the moments the
# player learns to dread, and they ignore the head-count target.
# kind    meaning
# swarm   <count> spawn together at one nearby point
# flock   <count> cross the whole viewport along one axis; get out of the way
# wall    a solid line advancing from one random edge
# ring    <count> spawn as a closing circle centred on the player; punch out
# elite   a bright, bold, HP-barred version with x20 hp; drops a chest
# tide    sustained: multiply the head-count target by <count> for 90s
# boss    the Countess. clock stops. she must die for the sun to rise.
beat
beat
column6: forty bats cross the screen on one axis

column1: beat
column6: THE WIGHT WALL — a line advances from one edge

column1: beat
beat
column6: THE RING — 60 ghouls close around you. punch out.

column1: beat
beat
beat
beat
beat
column6: THE TIDE — everything, every edge, 90 seconds

column1: beat
